behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` computes the numeric total of A1..A10 in a real worksheet context.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser -> range resolver -> function library -> evaluator handoff, not just SUM math. No OS/network/LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 to known values, set B1 to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, trigger evaluation, assert B1 equals expected total.

behaviors.description: Formula chaining across cells produces correct cascaded output (e.g., B1 depends on A1, C1 depends on B1).
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears in dependency resolution and cross-cell value propagation across modules; a single-function test can miss it.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`; evaluate workbook and assert B1=10 and C1=11.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency detection returns a circular-reference error instead of infinite evaluation.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection failure manifests when dependency graph construction and evaluator traversal interact. No external infra needed.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=`=B1+1` and B1=`=A1+1`, run evaluation with timeout guard, assert circular-reference error state for both cells and no hang.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact-match returns correct row value and not-found returns the expected error.
behaviors.justification: Needs interaction of function semantics with table/range extraction and value typing; pure unit on lookup helper can miss wiring bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup range, evaluate `=VLOOKUP(key, table, col, FALSE)` for existing and missing keys, assert correct value and not-found error.

behaviors.description: Nested IF expression selects correct branch for boundary values.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is expression/function evaluation logic inside one evaluator boundary; no required cross-module I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` against A1 values 5, 15, 25 and assert outputs low/medium/high.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is mostly function semantics (argument handling and empty-value policy) within local evaluation logic.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate CONCATENATE formulas over inputs including empty cells and assert separator placement has no extra blanks.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`Sheet2!A1`) resolves correctly when target sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Requires workbook/sheet namespace resolution plus evaluator integration; unit-level formula math is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Create Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1=7, set Sheet1!B1=`=Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate and assert Sheet1!B1=8.

behaviors.description: Dependent formulas recalculate after source-cell change in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in dependency graph invalidation/scheduling across modules; mocking one component can hide ordering bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build dependency chain and branching graph, evaluate once, mutate a root cell, trigger recalc, assert all downstream cells update to expected values (including branch nodes).

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores prior formula text and prior dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: Requires undo stack + formula storage + recalculation coordination. Single-module tests can miss state rehydration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Set baseline formulas/values, edit a formula causing downstream changes, execute undo, assert restored formula string and all affected dependent cell values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save/load roundtrip preserves formulas, values, and floating-point precision in custom binary format.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on serializer/deserializer + filesystem interaction. This is local file I/O (Integration), not external-system or AI behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and floating-point edge values, save to temp file, load into a new engine instance, assert formula text equality and numeric equality within strict precision tolerance.
